explanation: The natural language requirement states that the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (the volume of gas delivered per breathing cycle). The atomic propositions available include 'measureTV' for indicating measurement of tidal volume and 'displayTV' for indicating that the tidal volume is displayed. To ensure that for every breathing cycle, both measurement and display occur, we express that globally (for all time), whenever a breathing cycle starts ('breathingCycleStart'), it is eventually followed by both a measurement of tidal volume and a display of the measured tidal volume before the breathing cycle is done. Thus, the formula for each breathing cycle from start to done: whenever 'breathingCycleStart' occurs, until 'breathingCycleDone' occurs, both 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' must eventually occur within. The LTL property below captures this as: Globally, if a breathing cycle starts, then, before it is done, it is required that both the volume is measured and displayed at some point before the next cycle finishes. This property is the strongest over given atomic propositions and ensures both operations happen every cycle. No numerical comparison operators are used, only atomic propositions.

The natural language requirement states that the system must both measure and display the tidal volume (volume of gas delivered per breathing cycle). Among the atomic propositions, 'measureTV' corresponds to the measurement of tidal volume, and 'displayTV' corresponds to its display. The requirement applies to every breathing cycle, so for every occurrence of 'breathingCycleStart', there should subsequently be a measurement and display of TV. In LTL, this is captured by stating that globally, whenever a breathing cycle starts, then eventually 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' should both happen (either order is acceptable as the precise temporal ordering is not specified). This is expressed as: G(breathingCycleStart -> (F measureTV & F displayTV)). This property satisfies the system shall both measure and display tidal volume every breathing cycle and does not rely on unavailable atomic propositions such as a direct 'tidalVolumeMeasuredAndDisplayed'.

The natural language requirement specifies that for every breathing cycle, the system should both measure and display the tidal volume delivered to the patient. The available atomic propositions are 'measureTV' for measurement of tidal volume and 'displayTV' for displaying the tidal volume. The LTL property G(breathingCycleStart -> (F[0:1] (measureTV & displayTV))) expresses that: whenever a breathing cycle starts ('breathingCycleStart'), it is required that within the current or the next step (i.e., immediately after the start), both measurement and display of tidal volume ('measureTV & displayTV') occur. This captures the requirement that measuring and displaying tidal volume must happen during each breathing cycle. No other atomic propositions are related to tidal volume measurement or display, and previously used LTL patterns might fail to bind the actions to each cycle as needed, or might not ensure both measurement AND display happen in a timely context (per cycle).

The input requirement states that the system must measure and display the tidal volume (TV) delivered to the patient for each breathing cycle. The available atomic propositions 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' respectively capture the measurement and display aspects of tidal volume. The breathing cycle event is marked by 'breathingCycleStart'. Therefore, after every start of a breathing cycle, eventually the system must both measure and display the tidal volume. This is captured as: Globally, whenever 'breathingCycleStart' occurs, it is eventually followed by both 'measureTV' and 'displayTV'.

A property such as 'G (measureTV & displayTV)' would not adequately capture the requirement, because that would mean measurement and display happen all the time, not specifically associated to the breathing cycle. The proposed property tightly links the measurement and display to the occurrence of each breathing cycle start.

The input sentence requires that for every breathing cycle ('breathingCycleStart'), the system must measure and display the tidal volume delivered to the patient. The available atomic propositions include 'measureTV' (measure tidal volume) and 'displayTV' (display tidal volume). To capture this, we can use the LTL formula: globally, whenever a breathing cycle starts, it is eventually followed by both a measurement and a display of the tidal volume within the cycle. Formally, G(breathingCycleStart -> (F(measureTV) & F(displayTV))). This ensures that in every breathing cycle, both measurement and display of tidal volume are performed. Since the available propositions do not specify the exact order (whether display happens immediately after measurement), the formula only requires both happen eventually in the cycle. Previous formulae that may have used only one of 'measureTV' or 'displayTV' would not have fully captured the requirement, since the text demands both actions per cycle.

The input natural language requires that in every breathing cycle (‘per breathing cycle’), the system shall measure and display the tidal volume (volume of gas delivered to the patient). The atomic propositions provided include 'measureTV' for measurement and 'displayTV' for display of tidal volume, and 'breathingCycleStart' to indicate the start of each breathing cycle.

The correct LTL property should assert that for every breathing cycle (whenever 'breathingCycleStart' is true), eventually (within the cycle or soon after) both measurement and display of tidal volume occur. This is most succinctly captured as: Globally, whenever 'breathingCycleStart' holds, it is followed by (eventually) 'measureTV' AND (eventually) 'displayTV'.

Thus, the property directly expresses that measurement and display of tidal volume must happen in every breathing cycle. This avoids pitfalls of more general safety or liveness properties (e.g., "eventually measureTV" does not specify the per-cycle requirement, nor ties measurement to display or to cycle boundaries).

LTL:
G (breathingCycleStart -> (F measureTV & F displayTV))

The requirement specifies that for every breathing cycle, the system must measure and display the delivered tidal volume. From the given atomic propositions, 'breathingCycleStart' indicates the start of a breathing cycle, 'measureTV' indicates the measurement of tidal volume, and 'displayTV' indicates the display of tidal volume. The best way to represent that for each breathing cycle the tidal volume is both measured and displayed is to require that whenever a new breathing cycle starts (breathingCycleStart), then, in the future of this cycle, both the measurement and display of tidal volume will eventually happen before the next breathing cycle starts. This can be formalized in LTL as: 'Globally, whenever a breathing cycle starts, it is guaranteed that measureTV and displayTV will eventually happen, before the next breathing cycle starts.'

The LTL formula provided ensures that for every occurrence of 'breathingCycleStart', both 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' must occur before the next 'breathingCycleStart' (the start of the following cycle). Previous properties that might have only required 'measureTV' or 'displayTV' globally, or without conditioning on the breathing cycle, would have failed to capture the tight connection to the breathing cycle (i.e., it could happen at any time, not necessarily synchronized to the breathing cycle). The following property precisely expresses the natural language requirement.

The requirement states that in every breathing cycle, the system shall measure and display the tidal volume (TV). The atomic propositions available are 'breathingCycleStart', 'measureTV', and 'displayTV'. To capture this in LTL: whenever a new breathing cycle starts ('breathingCycleStart'), it should eventually be followed by events where TV is measured and displayed. This can be captured as: globally, whenever 'breathingCycleStart' occurs, eventually both 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' occur (not necessarily in immediate succession but within that breathing cycle). Thus, the formula is:

G (breathingCycleStart -> (F measureTV & F displayTV))

This ensures that for every instance of breathingCycleStart, TV measurement and display will follow.

Previous attempts that do not link the occurrences to each breathing cycle (for instance, only 'G (measureTV & displayTV)') might allow these actions to occur at arbitrary times, not necessarily for every breathing cycle, and thus would not satisfy the requirement for per-cycle completion.

The input natural language requirement states that the system shall measure and display the tidal volume (TV) for each breathing cycle. Based on the provided atomic propositions, 'measureTV' corresponds to measuring the tidal volume and 'displayTV' corresponds to displaying it. Therefore, for each breathing cycle that starts ('breathingCycleStart'), the system shall both measure and display the tidal volume during that cycle. In LTL, this can be expressed by saying that globally (at all times), whenever 'breathingCycleStart' is true, there will eventually (during that breathing cycle) follow a state where both 'measureTV' and 'displayTV' are true. This is captured as: G(breathingCycleStart -> F(measureTV & displayTV)). This property ensures that for each breathing cycle, both actions (measuring and displaying tidal volume) will happen at some point after the start of the cycle. No other previously given LTL expressions exist to compare or critique in this case.

The input statement requires that the system continuously measures and displays the volume of gas delivered to the patient per breathing cycle (tidal volume). The most appropriate atomic propositions provided are 'measureTV' (measurement of tidal volume) and 'displayTV' (displaying tidal volume). The requirements imply that for every breathing cycle, both measuring and displaying of tidal volume must occur. Since 'breathingCycleStart' indicates the beginning of a breathing cycle, a correct LTL property should ensure that whenever a breathing cycle begins, measurement and display of tidal volume eventually occur in that cycle. The LTL property 'G(breathingCycleStart -> F (measureTV & displayTV))' captures this: globally, whenever a new breathing cycle starts, eventually within that cycle, both measuring and displaying of tidal volume will be done. This ensures compliance with the requirement.
